metadata.title: All in a Row: Compressed Convolution Networks for Graphs
organizations: 1: Key Laboratory of Intelligent Information Processing, ICT, CAS, China
2: School of Computer Science and Technology, UCAS, China

sections.title: Motivation
content: Compared to Euclidean convolution, existing graph convolution methods generally fail to learn diverse convolution operators under limited parameter scales and depend on additional treatments of multi-scale feature extraction. In this paper, we want to bridge the gap between Euclidean space and graph space, and therefore generalize Euclidean convolution to graphs.

From the Euclidean convolution perspective, it is sensitive to the local spatial order, while GNNs should preserve permutation invariance, i.e., produce the same node representations regardless of the order of the nodes.

Irregular Local Structure visualization in Graph space

tables.description: Table of mathematical notations used in the paper

sections.title: Method
content: Regularization on Graphs:We propose to regularize graph-structured data through learnable permutations for end-to-end GNNs.Position Regression:Nodes with similar features or short paths in between will get closer position prediction.

Compressed Convolution Network:Learnable permutations decouple the input order and the follow-up module, therefore we can apply either permutation-sensitive or permutation-invariant operators on graphs.
Diagonal convolution learns both node features and structure features explicitly.
CoCNs directly apply hierarchical feature learning on graphs.
CoCNs use anti-diagonal compression to update structure features.

figures.description: Permutation Generation pipeline including Position Regression and Permutation Matrix
Detailed diagram of CoCN Layer and Convolution Module

tables.description: Graph Classification Results on MUTAG, PROTEINS, NCI1, IMDB, COLLAB datasets
Node Classification Results on Chameleon, Squirrel, Cornell, Texas, Wisconsin, Actor datasets

sections.title: Partial Model Study and Key Conclusions
content: Permutation module can permute input nodes with little information loss.
CoCNs learn complementary feature representations under different permutations.
CoCNs achieve better performance as the receptive field size increases.
Both node features and structure features generally contribute to the model prediction.
Poster presented at ICML 2023 in Hawaii.

figures.description: Ablation study plots showing accuracy vs number of permutations and average similarity vs epoch
